headline: France's national political crisis is taking root locally
description: The first round of the municipal elections, marked by low turnout and fragmented landscapes in big cities, confirms the depth of France's political crisis, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

headline: France ready to join Strait of Hormuz ship escorts once situation 'calmer,' Macron says
description: The French president emphasized nevertheless that 'we are not a party to the conflict and therefore France will never take part in operations to open or liberate the Strait of Hormuz.'

headline: Senior US counterterrorism official resigns to protest Iran war
description: In his resignation letter to Donald Trump, Joseph Kent said 'Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.'

headline: UAE's fishermen are collateral victims of the war in Iran
description: Since the start of the war in Iran, fishing boats have been confined to ports along the Emirati coastline near the Strait of Hormuz. In Dubai, the sector has come to a standstill and fish market prices have soared.

headline: Violence in restaurant kitchens exposes deep wounds in fine dining
description: The recent allegations against renowned Danish chef René Redzepi highlight the urgent need for a profound overhaul of management practices in the restaurant industry. Initiatives are spreading in France to break the silence surrounding abuse in the sector.

2026 municipal elections: See the first-round results on our map
description: Follow the 2026 municipal elections' first-round results in real time with our interactive map, and see which lists are in the lead and which mayors have been directly elected.
byline: Pierre Breteau,  Manon Romain and  Raphaëlle Aubert

headline: 2026 municipal election results: Key takeaways from a first round marked by low turnout
description: The second round will be less promising than hoped for the far right, despite significant gains in cities in the southeast, particularly in Marseille. Leading in Paris and Montpellier, the Socialists will need to clarify their positions regarding radical left candidates, who performed well.

headline: French court examines Yazidi genocide for first time in trial of jihadist
description: Sabri Essid is being tried in absentia, charged with 'genocide,' a first in France. Two women from the Iraqi minority will testify, recounting how they were enslaved.
